title: eriophorum gracile subsp. coreanum
intro: Eriophorum gracile subsp. coreanum, also known as the Korean cotton-grass, is a subspecies of the Eriophorum gracile plant, belonging to the family Cyperaceae and native to Korea and parts of Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots and rhizomes, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Eriophorum gracile subsp. coreanum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
